summary_one_line: Input must contain a syntactically valid SQL expression using the >= (TK_GE) relational operator so resolveExprStep dispatches into the TK_GE switch arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed SQL comparison expression whose operator parses to the TK_GE token (the two-character '>=' sequence) inside an expression that reaches resolveExprStep, so the switch( pExpr->op ) dispatches to the case TK_GE arm at line 98664.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT 11-013>=1 3=13...' — a dense, valid SQL expression that literally contains the '>=' (0x3e 0x3d) operator at offsets 0x0d-0x0e and is otherwise composed of digits, '-', '=', '<=', '/' that form parseable comparison expressions; the per-role overlay marks line 98664 [B]/[W] and DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows the winner takes case TK_GE T=1.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds contain comparison operators only sparsely and malformed ('SELECT ( 111111,...', 'SELECT+SE+...', 'SELECT ~~~~~44<4...'): they produce TK_LT ('<') or row-value parens/commas but never assemble a valid '>=' expression, so DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows the loser takes case TK_GE T=0 F=593 (never).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's integer-CMP interception writes a CMP_MAP bucket keyed by operand-distance: as the SQL tokenizer/parser compares input characters against operator-token codepoints and the parser state machine compares pExpr->op against TK_GE, value_profile's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to forming the '>=' token in a comparison context, giving a gradient that naive's plain edge-counter feedback cannot follow toward the rare TK_GE dispatch.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the TK_GE switch arm in resolveExprStep, the true side of which is globally blocked.
The blocked side is the true branch.
The TK_GE arm is one case of a switch over pExpr->op selecting the expression operator.
The winner-resolving seed is a valid SQL expression containing the '>=' operator.
The winning seed was found by value_profile.
The winner takes case TK_GE (true side) while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L98664  T=1 F=62  T=0 F=593  case TK_GE: {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser-blocking seeds are malformed SQL using '<' or row-value parens/commas, not a '>=' comparison.
A loser-blocking seed parses into row-value parenthesis/comma form rather than a binary comparison.
value_profile's CMP_MAP encodes operand closeness as a distance bucket, providing a matching gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were (Hamming distance bucket for `trace_cmp*`; matching-prefix
length for string/memory CMPs).

evidence_trail.claim: An input getting closer to matching a CMP operand pair is preserved as corpus, which is the gradient naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Decisive pair delta is value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient), not I2SRandReplace, so lineage/dash-row verification is not mandated; the loser is naive and the winner adds no I2S mutator. Attribution rests on the value_profile MECHANISM CONTEXT (Hamming/prefix-distance CMP_MAP feedback) plus the seed-byte evidence that the winner forms a valid '>=' comparison the naive loser never assembles.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the TK_GE/'>=' comparison arm in which value_profile shows no resolution advantage over naive (equal time-to-resolve across trials), indicating the CMP_MAP distance gradient does not drive reaching the comparison-operator dispatch.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is available, so the claim that the '>=' token specifically (rather than general valid-comparison-expression density) is the decisive feature rests on a single example.
